questionHtml:
A block of glass of mass 5 kg and temperature 30°C is brought into contact with a block of asphalt of mass 20 kg and temperature 75°C. The specific heat capacity of asphalt is twice that of glass. No energy is transferred to the surroundings. What is the final temperature of both blocks?


A.  35°C

B.  45°C

C.  60°C

D.  70°C

meta.title: A block of glass of mass 5 kg and temperature 30°C is brought into contact with a block of asphalt of mass 20 kg and temperature 75°C. The specific heat capacity of asphalt is twic
breadcrumbLabels: B. The particulate nature of matter
B. The particulate nature of matter
